title: General and operations managers
description: These managers run daily operations across retail, logistics, manufacturing, and services in the Philippines. They coordinate staffing, budgets, and compliance in firms with varying levels of digital adoption. AI will increasingly support planning and reporting, but local execution still depends heavily on in-person supervision.

title: Hotel and restaurant managers
description: Hospitality managers oversee hotels, resorts, and large food service establishments serving domestic and foreign demand. Work is customer-facing and operationally intensive, especially in tourism hubs. AI improves forecasting and customer support, but physical service delivery remains core.

title: Construction project managers
description: These managers lead building and infrastructure projects across public and private sectors. They coordinate contractors, permits, procurement, and site schedules under tight cost constraints. AI tools can support estimation and monitoring, but implementation remains field-based.

title: Registered nurses
description: Registered nurses provide bedside and community care in hospitals, clinics, and public health programs. The Philippines has a strong nurse migration channel, so foreign demand strongly affects domestic labor supply. AI assists documentation and triage, but hands-on care remains essential.

title: Public school teachers
description: Teachers deliver instruction in elementary and secondary schools across urban and rural areas. Their work combines classroom management, lesson planning, and community engagement. AI can help produce materials and assessments, but in-person teaching and supervision remain central.

title: Accountants
description: Accountants handle financial reporting, tax compliance, and audit support for firms and government entities. Much of the workflow is digital, standardized, and document-heavy. AI can automate substantial portions of reconciliation and reporting, especially in larger formal enterprises.

title: Software developers
description: Developers build applications for local enterprises, startups, and offshore clients. The occupation is highly digital and already uses AI coding assistants in production workflows. AI is likely to raise productivity quickly while changing team size, role mix, and required skills.

title: Civil engineers
description: Civil engineers design and supervise roads, housing, and water infrastructure in the Philippines. Their work blends software-based design with field inspection and contractor coordination. AI can speed design and compliance checks, but physical project delivery limits full automation.

title: Physicians and medical doctors
description: Doctors diagnose and treat patients across hospitals, clinics, and specialty practices. Decision-making uses medical data, but patient interaction and procedural care are deeply human and regulated. AI supports diagnostics and documentation, yet replacement risk is limited in the near term.

title: Medical laboratory technicians
description: Lab technicians process clinical samples and run diagnostic tests in hospitals and labs. The role is procedural, quality-controlled, and partially automated through instrumentation. AI can improve interpretation and workflow routing, but sample handling and regulatory oversight remain manual.

title: Electrical engineering technicians
description: These technicians install, test, and maintain electrical systems in buildings and industrial facilities. Tasks require site presence, instrumentation, and troubleshooting under variable conditions. AI can help diagnostics and documentation but cannot substitute physical maintenance work at scale.

title: Computer support specialists
description: IT support workers resolve device, network, and software issues for companies and public institutions. Many incidents are repetitive and documented, making parts of support workflows highly automatable. Complex troubleshooting and on-site maintenance still require human technicians.

title: Surveying and mapping technicians
description: Survey technicians gather and process field measurements for land, infrastructure, and cadastral projects. Their tasks combine geospatial software with physical site work. AI can improve mapping and error detection, but field capture remains labor-intensive.

title: BPO and call center agents
description: Call center agents handle customer service, sales, and back-office support for domestic and global clients. The occupation is highly digital, script-driven, and exposed to rapid AI progress in voice and text automation. Demand may shift toward more complex escalations and hybrid human-AI workflows.

title: Administrative clerks
description: Administrative clerks process records, prepare documents, and coordinate office transactions in government and private firms. Their duties involve significant routine information handling. AI can automate many document-centric tasks, though localized processes and legacy systems slow full substitution.

title: Bookkeeping clerks
description: Bookkeeping clerks record transactions, reconcile accounts, and prepare basic financial reports for MSMEs and larger firms. Much of the work follows repeatable templates and accounting software workflows. AI-enabled bookkeeping tools can reduce routine workload, especially in formal businesses.

title: Payroll clerks
description: Payroll clerks compute wages, deductions, and compliance contributions for public and private employers. The occupation is rules-based and increasingly software-mediated. AI can automate exception handling and validation, but legal accountability keeps humans in the loop.

title: Data encoding clerks
description: Data encoders input and normalize records for logistics, healthcare, retail, and government workflows. Tasks are highly routine and computer-based with clear performance metrics. This is one of the most directly automatable occupations through OCR, extraction, and AI form processing.

title: Retail salespersons
description: Retail sales workers assist customers and process transactions across malls, neighborhood stores, and informal stalls. The role is large in the Philippine labor market and spans both formal and informal establishments. AI affects inventory and customer analytics more than frontline selling in the near term.

title: Food service workers
description: Food service staff work in restaurants, eateries, and catering operations nationwide. Service tasks are physical and customer-facing, with high turnover and substantial informal employment. AI supports ordering and scheduling, but core preparation and service work remain labor-intensive.

title: Security guards
description: Security guards provide access control, patrol, and incident response in commercial, residential, and industrial sites. The occupation depends on physical presence and local situational judgment. Surveillance AI may alter monitoring workflows, but substitution is limited by legal and trust requirements.

title: Kasambahay (domestic workers)
description: Kasambahay provide household cleaning, caregiving, cooking, and errands in private homes. Employment is highly informal and relationship-based, with many workers outside formal contracts. Automation pressure is low in the Philippines due to affordability, trust, and technology deployment barriers.

title: Barangay health workers
description: Barangay health workers support local primary care, maternal health, and public health campaigns in communities. They bridge clinics and households through outreach and trust-based engagement. AI can assist records and triage, but community interaction remains the core value.

title: Hairdressers and beauty service workers
description: Beauty workers deliver grooming and cosmetic services in salons, clinics, and home-based setups. Services are manual and personalized, with high customer contact. AI can improve booking and marketing, but direct service tasks remain minimally automatable.

title: Rice and corn farmers
psoc_label: Skilled Agricultural, Forestry and Fishery Workers
description: Rice and corn farmers are central to rural livelihoods and food supply in the Philippines. Production depends on weather risk, land tenure, and local market access. AI can improve advisory services and input planning, but mechanized autonomy is constrained by farm scale and capital limits.

title: Vegetable and fruit growers
psoc_label: Skilled Agricultural, Forestry and Fishery Workers
description: These growers cultivate high-value crops for local consumption and inter-island trade. Work is seasonal, physical, and highly sensitive to logistics and spoilage. AI can support forecasting and pest detection, but labor-saving automation diffusion is slow in smallholder settings.

title: Livestock and poultry raisers
psoc_label: Skilled Agricultural, Forestry and Fishery Workers
description: Livestock and poultry workers manage feeding, health, sanitation, and market preparation in backyard and commercial operations. The occupation combines routine husbandry with disease and supply chain risks. AI helps monitoring and planning but cannot replace routine physical husbandry tasks in most settings.

title: Fishers and aquaculture workers
psoc_label: Skilled Agricultural, Forestry and Fishery Workers
description: Fishers and aquaculture workers support coastal and inland fisheries that feed local markets. Work is physically demanding and exposed to weather and fuel volatility. AI can aid route and yield planning, but real-world automation remains limited by vessel and infrastructure constraints.

title: Carpenters
description: Carpenters build and repair structures in residential, commercial, and infrastructure projects. Their work requires manual skill, measurement, and adaptation to site-specific conditions. AI can support estimation and planning, but direct craft execution remains human-led.

title: Masons and tile setters
description: Masons and tile setters are core to housing and public works construction in the Philippines. Work is physically intensive and performed in variable on-site environments. AI has limited direct substitution potential, though planning and materials management may improve.

title: Electricians
description: Electricians install and maintain wiring, controls, and safety systems in homes and industrial facilities. The occupation requires certification, site diagnostics, and compliance with local standards. AI can assist diagnostics and paperwork but cannot replace hands-on installation work.

title: Automotive mechanics
description: Automotive mechanics service jeepneys, motorcycles, cars, and fleet vehicles in formal shops and roadside garages. Work is manual, diagnostic, and dependent on local parts ecosystems. AI tools can support troubleshooting, but physical repair remains dominant.

title: Welders and flame cutters
description: Welders fabricate and repair metal structures for construction, shipyards, and factories. Many Filipino welders also participate in overseas labor channels. Robotic welding affects large formal plants first, while much local demand remains manual and project-based.

title: Jeepney drivers
psoc_label: Plant and Machine Operators and Assemblers
description: Jeepney drivers are a backbone of urban and peri-urban public transport in the Philippines. Work is route-based but deeply shaped by traffic, regulation, and daily fare dynamics. Autonomous replacement is unlikely in the near term, but platform and route optimization can reshape earnings.

title: Tricycle drivers
psoc_label: Plant and Machine Operators and Assemblers
description: Tricycle drivers provide short-distance mobility in towns and barangays where formal transit is limited. The occupation is highly informal and cash-based, with low technology penetration. AI affects matching and pricing only gradually through ride-hailing spillovers.

title: Delivery riders
psoc_label: Plant and Machine Operators and Assemblers
description: Delivery riders handle food, parcel, and e-commerce logistics, often mediated by platform apps. Their work is physical but tightly governed by algorithmic assignment and incentives. AI-driven dispatch, routing, and demand management can significantly reshape earnings and work intensity.

title: Bus and truck drivers
psoc_label: Plant and Machine Operators and Assemblers
description: Bus and truck drivers move passengers and goods across islands and corridors, supporting trade and commuting. Work is physically present, safety-critical, and constrained by road and fleet conditions. Full vehicle autonomy is unlikely soon, but fleet optimization and monitoring will expand.

title: Factory machine operators
psoc_label: Plant and Machine Operators and Assemblers
description: Machine operators run production lines in food processing, electronics, garments, and consumer goods. Tasks include monitoring, setup, and basic quality checks under time pressure. AI and automation can reduce routine roles in large plants, though smaller firms transition more slowly.

title: Construction laborers
description: Construction laborers perform material handling, site preparation, and support tasks across building projects. Employment is often project-based, seasonal, and partly informal. AI can optimize scheduling and procurement, but direct substitution of manual labor is limited in the near term.

title: Janitors and cleaners
description: Janitors and cleaners maintain offices, schools, malls, and households in both formal contracts and informal arrangements. Work is repetitive but physically distributed across varied environments. Cleaning robotics adoption in the Philippines remains constrained by cost and site conditions.

title: Street vendors and market sellers
description: Street vendors and market sellers are a major source of urban and peri-urban livelihoods. Transactions are highly informal, cash-heavy, and relationship-driven. AI has limited direct displacement potential, though digital payments and online commerce can gradually shift demand patterns.

title: Garbage collectors and sanitation workers
description: Sanitation workers collect and process waste under municipal and private contractors. Duties are physically demanding and often performed in challenging conditions. AI can improve route planning and sorting support, but frontline collection remains labor-intensive.

title: Porters and freight handlers
description: Porters and handlers move goods in markets, warehouses, ports, and transport terminals. Work is manual and strongly tied to e-commerce and distribution growth. AI and mechanization can change warehouse operations, but broad replacement is slowed by MSME logistics structures.

title: Seafarers and merchant marine ratings
description: Filipino seafarers are a globally significant workforce in international shipping and maritime services. Earnings are relatively high and heavily linked to overseas deployment channels. Automation risk can materialize abroad first through vessel digitization, reducing demand before domestic adjustment occurs.
